name: Homemade "Little Ears" Orecchiette Casalinga
recipeInstructions:
Source: 'The Good Cook - Pasta' Serves: Makes about 1 1/2 pounds fresh pasta, or 10-12 oz dried pasta
1. Combine the semolina, unbleached flour, and salt in a mound on a large work surface.
2. Make a well in the center with your finger and pour in 3 to 4 tablespoons water.
3. Gradually incorporate more flour from the inner wall of the well into the liquid, adding water as needed.
4. Continue forming a paste until the dough is smooth and elastic, taking about 20 minutes or so.
5. Form the dough into a ball and cover it with a damp cloth.
6. To make the "little ears," pull off a scant handful of the dough (keep the rest covered).
7. On a lightly floured board, roll the dough into a rope approximately 3/4 inch in diameter.
8. Cut the rope into slices no more than 1/8 inch thick to form small circles of dough.
9. Place one circle on your palm and use the thumb of the other hand to press and turn it at the same time, forming a dent in the center that will spread the dough slightly on each side. It should resemble a small ear with slightly thicker lobes.
10. Repeat with all remaining dough, placing the orecchiette on a lightly floured cloth as they are made.
11. Cook the orecchiette in the same manner as fresh flour and egg pasta, although they take longer to cook. Watch them carefully and taste frequently for doneness.
